samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>A traffic light system with red, yellow, and green indicators, and a pedestrian button.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Request signal for pedestrians to cross.</input>
        <output id="clock" width_description="[7:0]">Count value of the internal counter.</output>
        <output id="red">Red signal indicating the status of the traffic light.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow signal indicating the status of the traffic light.</output>
        <output id="green">Green signal indicating the status of the traffic light.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state_value id="idle">State value for idle state.</state_value>
        <state_value id="s1_red">State value for red state.</state_value>
        <state_value id="s2_yellow">State value for yellow state.</state_value>
        <state_value id="s3_green">State value for green state.</state_value>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="cnt" width_description="[7:0]">Internal counter for timing control.</register>
        <register id="state" width_description="[1:0]">Current state of the traffic light controller.</register>
        <register id="p_red">Previous value of the red signal.</register>
        <register id="p_yellow">Previous value of the yellow signal.</register>
        <register id="p_green">Previous value of the green signal.</register>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // State transition logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                state <= idle;
                p_red <= 0;
                p_yellow <= 0;
                p_green <= 0;
            end else begin
                case(state)
                    idle: begin
                        p_red <= 0;
                        p_yellow <= 0;
                        p_green <= 0;
                        state <= s1_red;
                    end
                    s1_red: begin
                        p_red <= 1;
                        p_yellow <= 0;
                        p_green <= 0;
                        if(cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                        end
                    end
                    s2_yellow: begin
                        p_red <= 0;
                        p_yellow <= 1;
                        p_green <= 0;
                        if(cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                        end
                    end
                    s3_green: begin
                        p_red <= 0;
                        p_yellow <= 0;
                        p_green <= 1;
                        if(cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        // Counting logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                cnt <= 10;
            end else begin
                if(pass_request && green) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else if(!green && p_green) begin
                    cnt <= 60;
                end else if(!yellow && p_yellow) begin
                    cnt <= 5;
                end else if(!red && p_red) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else begin
                    cnt <= cnt - 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assign statements for outputs
        assign red = p_red;
        assign yellow = p_yellow;
        assign green = p_green;
        
        // Assign statement for clock output
        assign clock = cnt;
    </implementation>
</module>
